evals.prompt: 我看到一篇关于 EEG-based non-invasive BCI 的新论文，帮我评估一下值不值得写
帮我建 electrode-hardware 这个子领域的知识库

analyst_observations: Skill provides massive quality improvement (+66.7% pass rate) at moderate token cost (~1.9x more tokens).
Biggest gap is in KB gap handling (Eval 2): skill correctly pauses and offers choice (4/4), baseline just plows ahead (0/4). This is the skill's most distinctive behavior.
Eval 1 (daily digest): both versions found the same story (NEO approval) and identified similar insights. Skill's advantage is structural — card format, dialogue invitation, KB gap warnings.

Eval 3 (KB construction): CRITICAL baseline failure — wrote files directly without user approval AND without web search verification. Self-acknowledged accuracy concerns. Skill correctly presented for approval first.
The one assertion the skill 'failed' on Eval 3 (template compliance) is actually expected — at the approval stage, full summaries haven't been generated yet.
Duration is comparable despite higher token usage, suggesting the skill's structured workflow doesn't add significant wall-clock time.
